title: Ceropegia clavicorona
taxonomy: Ceropegia clavicorona is classified within the Asclepiadaceae family, now commonly referred to as the Apocynaceae. This classification places it within the subfamily Periplocoideae. The species Ceropegia clavicorona belongs to the genus Ceropegia, which comprises approximately 130 species of flowering plants. These plants are often found in Africa and are known for their unique and varied appearances. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Ceropegia clavicorona shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as milkweed and oleander.
